title: The Donet
summary: The checked-in CME00008 XML is a local repository representation of University of Michigan CME item CME00008, manually keyed and proofread from Elsie Vaughan Hitchcock's 1921 EETS edition. Hitchcock transcribed the sole surviving Donet witness, Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 916, and collated parallel Part I passages against Pecock's separate abridgement The Poore Mennis Myrrour in British Library Add MS 37788. A complete scan of the 1921 edition and its scan metadata are cached. No public complete facsimile of either manuscript was found: Bodleian's current record explicitly says MS. Bodl. 916 is not available online, while the edition exposes only one monochrome manuscript plate, fol. 3v. Reproduction routes therefore request each whole physical codex and state the relevant textual portion separately.
review_notes: The Donet, The Poore Mennis Myrrour, both physical codices, the 1921 edition, the U-M encoding, and the repository artifact are distinct layers.
The exact Donet text occupies MS. Bodl. 916 fols. 1r-106v. The current catalog's fols. 1r-109v locus is explicitly inferred for the sole msItem and includes blank matter after the written text.
The Poore Mennis Myrrour is a separate authorial abridgement of Part I, not a second witness of the complete Donet; Add MS 37788 is therefore a collation source rather than a Donet base witness.
Public access, local caching, copyright, image-service terms, and permission to redistribute or use images commercially are recorded separately.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00008
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify the file as CME00008.
The 2016 TEMPHEAD change records structural and proofing work, not the medieval work's date or necessarily its first electronic publication.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00008
entities.description: A public TEI-like digital text keyed from Hitchcock's 1921 public-domain edition, including the edition title page, editor's summary, surviving Donet text, page and folio milestones, and embedded editorial notes.

date_statements.label: repository XML review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD change date documents a review stage in this representation.

notes: The collection history says the 2016-2018 batch was manually keyed and proofread from public-domain editions. The 2016 review date and phase-3 location strongly associate CME00008 with that batch, but no item-level production log was located.

entities.label: Hitchcock's 1921 edition of The Donet

bibliographic.citation: Reginald Pecock, The Donet, now first edited from MS. Bodl. 916 and collated with The Poore Mennis Myrrour (British Museum, Addl. 37788), ed. Elsie Vaughan Hitchcock, Early English Text Society, Original Series 156 (London: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1921).
bibliographic.title: The Donet
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 156
bibliographic.publisher: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, for the Early English Text Society

notes: The surviving Donet text occupies printed pp. 1-214. The frontispiece, preface, introduction, notes, Poore Mennis Myrrour appendix, biblical-reference list, glossary, and index are separate reproduction or editorial matter.
The title page says 'now first edited,' supporting 1921 as the first printed edition of the complete surviving work.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 916
entities.description: The sole known surviving manuscript of The Donet, a parchment codex copied fair in the second half of the fifteenth century and corrected by another hand. The Donet text fills the 106 written folios; blank and shortened leaves follow it.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: second half of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: Thomas Allen gave the manuscript to the Bodleian collection in 1601; the old Arch. B. I. designation remains on fol. 1r.

physical_description.extent: Current catalog: iii + 109 leaves; Hitchcock: 106 written folios followed by blank and shortened leaves
notes: The exact written work runs fols. 1r-106v; the current msItem locus 1r-109v is marked inferred and extends through blank matter.
Two leaves are missing after fol. 76 and two after fol. 82. Hitchcock also identifies a smaller discontinuity between fols. 56v and 57r.
Spaces for illuminated initials remain unfilled, with guide letters visible. Red writing marks selected structural sentences.
The manuscript was written fair by one apparently consistent scribe and corrected by another hand; later marginal notes and ownership additions also survive.
No public complete or partial Digital Bodleian object for this shelfmark was located as of 2026-07-11; MARCO explicitly labels it not available online.
Hitchcock's 1921 volume reproduces only fol. 3v as a monochrome frontispiece. A complete reproduction request must not treat that plate as manuscript coverage.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 37788
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century miscellany whose first textual component, The Poore Mennis Myrrour on fols. 3r-63v, is Pecock's separate abridgement of Part I of the Donet. Hitchcock used its parallel wording for collation but did not treat it as a copy of the complete Donet.

historical_designations: Amherst manuscript sold as lot 676 in 1909
holding.notes: Acquired by the British Museum in 1909; the current British Library component record is authoritative for the shelfmark and access route.

physical_description.extent: iv + 80 leaves in Hitchcock's cited British Museum catalog description
notes: The Poore Mennis Myrrour occupies fols. 3r-63v; fol. 15r is blank and partially cut, but belongs within its sequence.
The remaining leaves contain other English and Latin theological, moral, poetic, and practical material, so a work-only excerpt would not be a complete codex facsimile.
The relevant association to CME00008 is the related abridgement on fols. 3r-63v, not a claim that The Donet itself occupies those leaves.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Hitchcock's 1921 edition
entities.description: A complete 314-page digital scan of the printed EETS volume, including its manuscript plate, preliminaries, edition text, notes, appendices, glossary, index, and advertisements.
entities.reproduction_format: Scanned page images rendered as PDF with OCR and scan-structure metadata
notes: The Donet text maps to printed pp. 1-214 and Internet Archive leaves n39-n252.

entities.label: Hitchcock frontispiece of MS. Bodl. 916, fol. 3v
entities.description: A single monochrome printed plate reproducing fol. 3v of the manuscript, preserved as the frontispiece of the 1921 edition and visible in the complete Internet Archive scan.
entities.reproduction_format: Monochrome printed facsimile plate within a scanned book
notes: This is one selected manuscript surface, not a complete or representative whole-codex facsimile.

entities.label: Bodleian TEI catalog description of MS. Bodl. 916
entities.description: The machine-readable institutional description used for current shelfmark, contents, date, origin, physical structure, condition, provenance, and surrogate information.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: The official duration guidance used for the U.S. status of the 1921 edition.

object.description: University of Michigan CME item CME00008

scope.notes: Matching identifiers, title matter, chapter hierarchy, page and folio sequence, text, and notes support the relation; the exact transfer date and any repository normalization are undocumented.

subject.description: The complete U-M CME00008 electronic text

object.description: Edition title page, editor's summary of contents, and surviving Donet text with inline footnotes and marginal summaries

scope.notes: The encoding excludes the frontispiece pixels, preface, introduction, separate notes section, Poore Mennis Myrrour appendix, biblical-reference list, glossary, index, and advertisements.

object.description: The 106 written folios carrying The Donet

scope.notes: The text is interrupted by lost leaves after fols. 76 and 82 and a smaller discontinuity between 56v and 57r. Hitchcock expands abbreviations, adds punctuation, supplies and emends readings in brackets, and documents manuscript forms in notes.

subject.description: Part I parallel passages printed with Poore Mennis Myrrour variants and Appendix I

object.description: The Poore Mennis Myrrour, a separate abridgement of Part I

scope.notes: The relation records Hitchcock's editorial comparison. It does not make Add MS 37788 a witness of the complete Donet or a source for Part II.
The plate does not reproduce the rest of the codex, its binding, blanks, missing-leaf stubs, rubrication in color, or other surfaces.

access.access_method: Persistent institutional record, MARCO extract, or pinned machine-readable TEI source

local_copies.label: Pinned Bodleian TEI catalog description for MS. Bodl. 916

work_portion.label: The Donet
notes: The catalog locus is explicitly inferred because this is the sole msItem; Hitchcock identifies 106 written folios and blank matter after the work, so the exact text boundary is fol. 106v.
This local copy contains catalog metadata, not manuscript images.

access.format: HTML and TEI XML catalog metadata

access.access_method: Exact shelfmark, title, author, MARCO, Medieval Manuscripts, and Digital Bodleian data-API searches
notes: MARCO explicitly says 'Not available online.' Digital Bodleian returned no author or title match for this item. This date-bounded result does not prove that no internal preservation photography or unindexed surrogate exists.
The selected fol. 3v plate in the 1921 edition is recorded separately and must not be mistaken for a complete facsimile.

access.access_method: Register for Bodleian Archives & Manuscripts Requests, request MS. Bodl. 916 from its catalog record, and consult it in the Weston Library Level 1 reading room subject to preservation and access review
Email the completed mediated-copying form to mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk and request the complete physical codex, not only the written Donet folios

local_copies.label: Bodleian Libraries mediated copying order form

access.format: Reference-quality PDF scans supplied by file transfer, if the entire object is suitable for copying
notes: Specify front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all preliminary and final flyleaves, every recto and verso including fols. 1r-106v, all blanks and shortened leaves, and visible missing-leaf stubs or inserts. Ask for the authoritative image sequence and foliation.
Map The Donet separately to fols. 1r-106v; do not treat the catalog-inferred 1r-109v range as a verified text boundary.
The current form lists GBP 1 per scan and a GBP 10 minimum for non-Oxford users, but the item is subject to conservation, legal, and capacity review and needs an item-specific quote.
Reference-copy supply does not authorize Git redistribution or commercial print-on-demand use.

access.access_method: Submit the Imaging Services image-order form for complete high-resolution color coverage, subject to preservation review and a negotiated large-order quotation

local_copies.label: Bodleian Imaging Services image order form
Bodleian image order terms and conditions
Bodleian Imaging Services terms of use

access.format: High-resolution color TIFF images; large orders negotiated individually
notes: Request the same complete physical coverage specified for mediated copying, with The Donet mapped separately to fols. 1r-106v.
The 2026 form lists high-resolution color TIFF rates but the public page says large orders are negotiated; this manifest does not estimate a final whole-codex price.
Ask imaging@bodleian.ox.ac.uk in writing whether supplied images may be redistributed in this repository or used in commercial print-on-demand outputs.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue component record
Exact shelfmark and title searches in the current British Library catalogue and public digitized-manuscript routes
notes: The item record has no digitized-content link. This does not establish that no internal surrogate or unindexed image set exists.

access.access_method: Obtain or renew a British Library Reader Pass, request Add MS 37788 through the online collection item request form, and consult it in London subject to availability
Submit the British Library online image order for complete physical-codex coverage, request color explicitly, and ask whether an existing complete surrogate can be supplied
access.contact: permissions@bl.uk for written reuse permission; the online form handles the image order
access.format: Item-specific reference images or reproduction-quality photography; original manuscripts require photography rather than ordinary scanning
notes: Request front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso through the entire miscellany, and any inserts or stubs, rather than only fols. 3r-63v.
Associate the supporting source to CME00008 as The Poore Mennis Myrrour on fols. 3r-63v, a related abridgement used for collation; do not label those leaves as The Donet.
Ask permissions@bl.uk before redistributing supplied images or using them in a commercial print-on-demand workflow.

local_copies.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright

rights.component: The CME00008 digital text and materials presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The U-M item and collection pages state that the materials are in the public domain and apply the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: No Copyright-United States is jurisdiction-specific and is not a worldwide license.
It does not automatically govern the Internet Archive scan or newly supplied Bodleian or British Library manuscript images.

rights.component: Upstream U-M transcription and 1921 source content represented in the local XML
rights.basis: The local artifact is supported as a representation of U-M CME00008, which U-M marks No Copyright-United States, and its immediate edition was published in 1921.
notes: This does not determine rights in project-authored metadata, software, typography, generated PDFs, or future editorial additions.

rights.component: The 1921 transcription, introduction, editorial notes, appendices, glossary, and index
rights.basis: U-M expressly marks the CME materials derived from this edition public domain in the United States. The edition was published in 1921, and U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A explains the 95-year maximum term for works already under statutory protection before 1978; no contrary U.S. status was found.
notes: This U.S. assessment does not decide every other jurisdiction or the contractual terms of a particular scan provider.

rights.component: Internet Archive's digital surrogate files and item presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider statement and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata exposes a public download but supplies no item-level rights or license field. Public access does not itself grant a reuse license.
notes: The underlying 1921 edition is separately assessed as public domain in the United States.

rights.component: The fifteenth-century Donet text embodied in the manuscript
rights.basis: The work and witness are fifteenth-century, and U-M expressly marks its modern public transcription No Copyright-United States.
notes: This concerns the historical text, not ownership of the physical object, modern photography, catalog text, or image-service terms.

rights.component: Any new reference scans supplied through Bodleian mediated copying
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The current mediated-copying form says scans of out-of-copyright material are copyright Bodleian Libraries and supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0; the service and terms preserve item-level and use-specific restrictions.
notes: A repository supporting for-profit or otherwise commercial print-on-demand activity cannot assume that CC BY-NC authorizes repository redistribution or downstream commercial use. Obtain written item-specific permission first.
The medieval text's age does not erase terms attached to newly supplied image files.

rights.component: Any high-resolution color images supplied by Bodleian Imaging Services
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: Bodleian's forms and terms distinguish private or academic non-commercial use from commercial use, require attribution and applicable notices, and direct commercial licensing questions to Imaging Services. No item-specific image license exists before an order is fulfilled.
notes: Retain the order confirmation and exact written reuse grant. Do not commit supplied images or place them in commercial outputs unless the grant expressly permits those acts.

rights.component: Modern photographs, scans, or surrogate images supplied through British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services makes the customer responsible for third-party rights and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material, potentially with additional fees.
notes: Permission to receive or study a reproduction is not permission to publish it in a Git repository or commercial print-on-demand product.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters are printed in italics, with stated conventions for ambiguous er/ir and other signs
editorial_practices.description: Hitchcock explicitly marks manuscript abbreviation expansions typographically rather than presenting italic letters as authorial emphasis.

editorial_practices.representation: Punctuation is mainly editorial and intentionally heavier for readability
editorial_practices.description: The edition's punctuation should not be treated as a diplomatic image of manuscript pointing.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Obvious manuscript errors, marginal corrections, lacunae, blank references, and uncertain readings
editorial_practices.representation: Editorial emendations, supplied references, and marginal corrections appear in square brackets with manuscript readings or explanations in notes
editorial_practices.description: Bracketed text belongs to a documented editorial layer and must be checked against the witness before it is treated as literal manuscript writing.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Parallel wording in Pecock's separate Poore Mennis Myrrour abridgement
editorial_practices.representation: Substantive variants are collated in Donet pp. 27-101 footnotes; materially different passages and a correspondence table are printed in Appendix I
editorial_practices.description: Orthographic, capitalization, word-division, and many grammatical differences were deliberately omitted from the collation, while substantive differences were retained.
editorial_practices.scope: Part I parallel passages and Appendix I, pp. 223-230

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete printed volume containing modern preliminaries, a manuscript plate, the edition text, and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: The XML encodes the title page, editor's summary, and Donet pp. 1-214 with inline notes, but omits the plate pixels, introduction, separate notes, appendices, glossary, index, and advertisements
editorial_practices.description: The U-M object is complete for the surviving work text as selected from the edition, not a complete textual or visual encoding of every page in the printed volume.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hitchcock's italic typography for expanded manuscript abbreviations
editorial_practices.representation: Italic spans are retained as HI REND='italic' throughout the encoded work
editorial_practices.description: Many XML italic spans represent expanded abbreviations rather than emphasis; manuscript letterforms cannot be reconstructed from the normalized encoding alone.
editorial_practices.scope: Donet text, including 14,114 HI elements within the treatise division

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME00008.xml.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; EEBO/IDG; TEXT/FRONT; TEXT/BODY/DIV1[@TYPE='treatise']
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00008 and its 1921 source edition, encodes the edition title and summary plus the surviving prologue and two-part Donet, preserves printed-page and manuscript-folio milestones, and represents editorial notes and italic expansions.

evidence.citation: Reproducible XPath and structural inspection of CME00008.xml.
evidence.locator: ETS/EEBO/TEXT/BODY/DIV1[@TYPE='treatise']
evidence.summary: Counts one prologue, 18 Part I DIV3 chapters, 23 Part II DIV3 chapters, 642 P, 214 PB, 2,299 NOTE, 14,114 HI, and no L or LG elements. It also confirms that manuscript milestones run from 1r/1a through 106v/106b.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item CME00008.
evidence.locator: item information, rights statement, contents, and full text
evidence.summary: Identifies Pecock, Hitchcock's 1921 edition, and doctrinal theology; exposes the prologue and two-part chapter hierarchy; and applies No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection description and rights statement.
evidence.locator: collection history and Rights and Permissions
evidence.summary: States that the 2016-2018 batch was manually keyed from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and basic TEI semantics and that collection materials are public domain in the United States.

evidence.citation: Reginald Pecock, The Donet, ed. Elsie Vaughan Hitchcock, EETS Original Series 156 (London: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1921).
evidence.locator: frontispiece; title page; introduction pp. xi-xxiv; text pp. 1-214; notes pp. 215-222; Appendix I pp. 223-230
evidence.summary: The immediate edition describes the Bodl. 916 codex and lacunae, argues a 1443-1449 date range, states its transcription conventions, prints the surviving work, and documents the Poore Mennis Myrrour comparison and Add MS 37788.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scandata for donetpeco00peco.
evidence.locator: leafNum 39/pageNumber 1 through leafNum 252/pageNumber 214
evidence.summary: Maps the edition's printed Donet pp. 1-214 to Internet Archive leaves n39-n252 and records 314 scanned leaves for the complete digital object.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata API record for donetpeco00peco.
evidence.locator: metadata, file list, and rights/license fields
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1921 volume and its public files but supplies no item-level rights or license value for the digital surrogate.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Bodl. 916 (manuscript_1880).
evidence.locator: msIdentifier; msContents/msItem 1; physDesc; history; surrogates
evidence.summary: Supplies current identity, an explicitly inferred 1r-109v locus, sole-witness status, language, parchment support, extent, missing leaves, English origin and date, provenance, and the fol. 3v printed surrogate.

evidence.citation: Manuscripts and Archives at Oxford University, MS. Bodl. 916 extract record.
evidence.locator: identity, contents, requesting, full-record link, and online availability
evidence.summary: Identifies the codex and Donet, exposes the institutional request route, and explicitly labels the item 'Not available online.'

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian data API and public search for MS. Bodl. 916, The Donet, and Reginald Pecock.
evidence.locator: exact-title and author searches; data-API totalItems and member records
evidence.summary: Returned no matching Digital Bodleian object for The Donet or Reginald Pecock; shelfmark-token searches returned unrelated 916 records. Combined with MARCO's item-level label, this supports only the dated negative public-facsimile finding.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of Hitchcock's frontispiece reproducing Bodleian MS. Bodl. 916, fol. 3v.
evidence.locator: Internet Archive leaf n7; PDF page 8; manuscript fol. 3v
evidence.summary: Confirms one legible monochrome printed manuscript plate. It does not expose color or any other codex surface.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Bodleian Archives & Manuscripts Requests.

open_questions.question: Does the Bodleian hold an existing complete color surrogate of MS. Bodl. 916, and can a complete physical-codex copy be supplied with terms permitting repository redistribution and possible commercial print-on-demand use?
next_steps: Ask mediated copying and Imaging Services whether a complete surrogate already exists and whether the entire codex is suitable for new imaging.
Request every physical surface, including binding, pastedowns, flyleaves, fols. 1r-106v, final blanks and shortened leaves, and visible stubs; request color and authoritative sequence metadata.
Obtain written item-specific reuse permission before committing any supplied image or using it in a commercial output.
If supplied, cache one complete deliverable or auditable full-image bundle and map The Donet separately to fols. 1r-106v.

open_questions.question: How should the catalog-inferred fols. 1r-109v msItem range be reconciled image by image with Hitchcock's 106 written folios and the later blank or shortened leaves?
next_steps: Use a complete image sequence or an institutional response to identify every written, blank, shortened, and unfoliated surface.
Retain fols. 1r-106v as the text mapping unless direct image or catalog evidence establishes a different written boundary.

open_questions.question: Does the British Library hold an existing complete surrogate of Add MS 37788, and under what terms could it be supplied and reused?
next_steps: Ask Imaging Services for whole-codex color coverage rather than only Poore Mennis Myrrour fols. 3r-63v.
Map the relevant supporting component as the separate abridgement on fols. 3r-63v and preserve all adjacent miscellany leaves in the complete cache.
Obtain written permission before repository redistribution or commercial use.

open_questions.question: Can an item-level U-M production record confirm CME00008's exact keying, proofreading, and release history within the 2016-2018 batch?
next_steps: Ask U-M for item-level production metadata or a raw bulk-distribution record for CME00008.
Keep the collection-level manual-keying statement qualified until item-level confirmation is found.
